misconceptionSummary: Students include internal shared edges in the perimeter instead of only counting the outer boundary.

visuals.ruleText: Perimeter = the outer boundary only — internal edges where shapes join are NOT included
visuals.subText: Trace the outside of the compound shape with a finger to identify every boundary edge

steps.narration: A compound shape is two or more shapes joined together. The perimeter is only the outer edge — the boundary you would walk around. Any edge where two component shapes share a join is an internal edge and must not be counted.
Calculate unlabelled boundary edges before adding. Use the overall dimensions minus the cut-out dimensions — as with any rectilinear compound shape.
Add only the outer boundary edges. Six boundary sides: ten, eight, six, three, four, five. Total thirty-six centimetres.

visuals.label: Trace boundary → calculate missing sides → add outer edges only

steps.narration: Thirty-six centimetres. Trace the outside, find any missing lengths by subtraction, then add every outer edge.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: including the internal join edge in the perimeter. Only the outer boundary counts — trace the shape with a finger to check every edge you include is on the outside.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Internal edges where shapes join are not part of the perimeter. Only the outer boundary counts. Trace the outside edge to identify exactly which lengths to add.
